title: Joint Realizability Tradeoffs Bounded by Quantum Channel Incompatibility
authors: Shintaro Minagawa, Ryo Takakura, Kensei Torii
abstract: Incompatible quantum channels cannot be jointly and exactly realized, meaning that any approximate joint realization inevitably entails a tradeoff in implementation accuracy. While this notion of channel incompatibility unifies fundamental limitations such as measurement uncertainty, the no information without disturbance principle, and the no-cloning and no-broadcasting theorems, connecting these traditional relations directly to the resource-theoretic strength of incompatibility has remained elusive. In this Letter, we show that generalized robustness, a typical resource quantifier of channel incompatibility, lower bounds the total error of any approximate joint realization. Applying this result to measurement channels provides a unified, model-independent framework encompassing error-error and information-error-disturbance tradeoffs. Furthermore, our robustness-based evaluation of disturbance outperforms an algebraic bound for all POVMs in dimensions up to six.
summary: This paper demonstrates that the robustness of quantum channel incompatibility acts as a fundamental lower bound on the error incurred when trying to jointly realize incompatible operations. By doing so, it provides a unified mathematical framework that encompasses Heisenberg-type uncertainty relations and information-disturbance trade-offs. The new bounds are shown to be tighter than existing algebraic bounds in low-dimensional systems.
main_problem: Establishing a quantitative connection between the resource-theoretic strength of quantum channel incompatibility and the fundamental physical trade-offs in their approximate joint realization.
main_result: The authors prove that the generalized robustness of incompatibility (RoI) provides a universal lower bound on the total error of any approximate joint realization of incompatible channels, unifying various uncertainty and no-go theorems.
method: The study utilizes Semidefinite Programming (SDP), the Choi-Jamiołkowski isomorphism, and Lagrangian duality to derive bounds and quantify the robustness of quantum resources.
model_or_system: The framework involves finite-dimensional Hilbert spaces, quantum channels (CPTP maps), and Positive Operator-Valued Measures (POVMs), specifically examining measurement channels and instruments.
key_observables: Generalized Robustness of Incompatibility (RoI), diamond norm error, measurement disturbance, and information-error-disturbance trade-offs.
important_parameters: Dimension of the Hilbert space (d), approximation errors (epsilon), and the robustness parameter (r).
main_assumptions: The maps are assumed to be Completely Positive and Trace-Preserving (CPTP); the derivation of certain bounds assumes a reference system of dimension equal to the system dimension.
figures_summary: Figure 1 illustrates the approximate joint realization tradeoff with error bounds; Figure 2 compares the new robustness-based bound against the Heinosaari-Miyadera bound, showing it is tighter for low dimensions; Figure 3 compares bounds for a specific 6-element POVM.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of channel incompatibility, presents a universal theorem for joint realization trade-offs, extends these results to measurement uncertainty and information-disturbance relations, and provides a detailed mathematical appendix with SDP derivations and proofs.
why_interesting: This work provides a unified, model-independent framework for understanding fundamental quantum limits, which is highly relevant for researchers working on the precision and stability of quantum measurements and open quantum systems.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 1. Approximate joint realization tradeoff of two quan- tum channels. A joint realization channel ΘA→B1B2 induces two marginal channels Θ(1) A→B1 and Θ(2) A→B2, which approx- imate the target channels ΛA→B1 and ΞA→B2 with errors ϵ1 := ∥ΛA→B1 −Θ(1) A→B1∥⋄and ϵ2 := ∥ΞA→B2 −Θ(2) A→B2∥⋄ respectively. Theorem 1 shows 2R(ΛA→B1, ΞA→B2) ⩽ϵ1 + ϵ2.
FIG. 2. Comparison with the robustness-based bound 2(√1 + η −1)2 and Heinosaari–Miyadera’s bound η2/16 for the unbiased qubit POVM Eq. (6).
FIG. 3. Comparison with our robustness-based bound 2(√2 −p −1)2 and Heinosaari–Miyadera’s bound (1 −p)2/144 for the POVM Eq. (S11).
